Start each Claude Code session by (1) revisiting your scope document and (2) providing the model with your CLAUDE.md architectural context document. End each session by updating it with any decisions the session surfaced. The goal is a codebase whose structure you can explain, not just a codebase that runs.
每次 Claude Code 会话开始时，要（1）重新审视你的范围文档，以及（2）向模型提供你的 CLAUDE.md 架构背景文档。在每次会话结束时，用会话中浮现的任何决策来更新它。目标是得到一个你能解释其结构的代码库，而不仅仅是一个能运行的代码库。
Exercise: Create a simple session template for your Claude Code work that includes the architectural context document, the specific task for this session, and any constraints or patterns to observe. At the end of each session, add a brief log entry to the context document that details what was built, what decisions were made, and what assumptions the session introduced. Five minutes of documentation per session is cheap insurance against architectural drift that compounds into an unmanageable codebase.
练习：为你的 Claude Code 工作创建一个简单的会话模板，其中包括架构背景文档、本次会话的具体任务，以及任何需要遵守的约束或模式。在每次会话结束时，向背景文档中添加一条简短的日志条目，详细说明构建了什么、做出了什么决策，以及本次会话引入了哪些假设。每次会话五分钟的文档记录，是对抗会累积成无法管理的代码库的架构漂移的廉价保险。
Security review before any user touches it 在任何用户接触之前进行安全审查As an AI-native startup founder, your responsibility is to know what’s in your codebase, understand any potential exposure vectors, and not ship obvious vulnerabilities to real users who are trusting you with their data.
作为人工智能原生初创企业的创始人，你的责任是了解你的代码库中有什么，理解任何潜在的风险暴露路径，并且不向那些信任你、将数据托付给你的真实用户发布明显的漏洞。
Claude can do a useful first-pass security review of AI-generated code and can help identify common vulnerabilities. It’s a good habit to build into the loop before shipping. It is not a substitute for security tooling, however, or, at higher stakes, a human reviewer—and founders who treat it as one are the ones who end up in the breach stories.
Claude 可以对人工智能生成的代码进行有用的初轮安全审查，并帮助识别常见的漏洞。这是在发布前融入工作流程的一个好习惯。然而，它不能替代安全工具，或者在风险更高的情况下，不能替代人工审查者——那些将其视为替代品的创始人，最终会成为数据泄露故事的主角。
Claude Code Security goes further: it scans codebases for security vulnerabilities and suggests targeted patches for human review, surfacing issues that traditional methods can miss.
Claude Code Security 走得更远：它扫描代码库中的安全漏洞，并提出有针对性的补丁供人工审查，揭示出传统方法可能遗漏的问题。
Note: At the time of this ebook’s publication, Claude Code Security is a limited beta release so check current availability before building it into your workflow.
注意：在本电子书出版时，Claude Code Security 是一个限量测试版，因此在将其融入你的工作流程之前，请检查当前的可用性。
Exercise: Before deploying to any real users, run your core application code through Claude with a specific brief: review for authentication and session handling, data exposure in API responses, input validation and injection risks, and dependencies with known vulnerabilities. Treat each finding seriously and assess whether it requires a fix, with human review for anything that touches authentication, secrets, or data handling.
练习：在部署给任何真实用户之前，用 Claude 运行你的核心应用代码，并附带一个具体的指令：审查身份验证和会话处理、API 响应中的数据暴露、输入验证和注入风险，以及带有已知漏洞的依赖项。认真对待每一个发现，评估是否需要修复，并对任何涉及身份验证、密钥或数据处理的内容进行人工审查。
Build your measurement framework before launch 在发布前建立你的测量框架The founders who mis-identify early traction as product-market fit are typically the same ones who started tracking data after launch, using metrics chosen to assess what was working rather than to surface what wasn’t. The antidote is to establish your measurement framework before the first user shows up.
那些将早期吸引力误认为是产品-市场契合的创始人，通常也是那些在发布后才开始跟踪数据的人，他们选择的指标是为了评估哪些有效，而不是揭示哪些无效。解药是在第一个用户出现之前就建立你的测量框架。
Use Claude to define which metrics matter for your specific product, what the benchmarks are, and what patterns in the data would constitute genuine product-market fit versus flattering noise. Specifically: set your retention benchmarks, your activation criteria, and your Day 7 and Day 30 targets before releasing your MVP.
使用 Claude 来定义哪些指标对你的特定产品很重要，基准是什么，以及数据中的哪些模式构成了真正的产品-市场契合，哪些只是奉承的噪音。具体来说：在发布你的 MVP 之前，设定你的留存基准、激活标准，以及第 7 天和第 30 天的目标。
Next, define what a false positive looks like for your specific product: signups without activation, revenue without retention, or initial enthusiasm without repeat usage, for example. When the data arrives, ask Claude to make the adversarial case against your own traction: what would a skeptic say about these numbers?
接下来，为你的特定产品定义什么是假阳性：例如，没有激活的注册、没有留存的收入，或者没有重复使用的初始热情。当数据到来时，要求 Claude 对你自己的吸引力提出反驳：一个怀疑论者会对这些数字说什么？
Manage discovery and user feedback logistics 管理客户探索和用户反馈的后勤工作Once real users are in the product, the operational layer expands fast. Claude Cowork handles the important-but-tedious work like building and maintaining user contact lists, running outreach sequences, scheduling feedback sessions, triaging bug reports, and tracking iteration cycles. The same MCP integrations that managed discovery logistics in the Idea stage apply here.
一旦真实用户进入产品，运营层就会迅速扩大。Claude Cowork 处理重要但繁琐的工作，如构建和维护用户联系人列表、运行触达序列、安排反馈会议、分类错误报告以及跟踪迭代周期。在创意阶段管理客户探索后勤的相同 MCP 集成也适用于此处。
Keep a human in the collection loop for nuanced exploration of user feedback. A user saying, for example, “this is great but I wish it could also…” requires interpretation: Is it a core need or a nice-to-have? Is it specific to this customer or representative of a segment? Is the missing feature the real problem, or is something upstream in the onboarding? No tool can answer those questions.
在收集循环中保留人类，以便对用户反馈进行细微的探索。例如，一个用户说“这很棒，但我希望它还能……”，这需要解读：这是一个核心需求还是一个锦上添花的功能？这是特定于这个客户，还是代表了一类用户？缺失的功能是真正的问题，还是入职流程中上游的某个环节出了问题？没有任何工具能回答这些问题。
Exercise: Configure Claude Cowork to run your MVP-stage feedback loop: draft outreach to your early user list, schedule feedback sessions, design structured intake process for bug reports and feature requests, and write up a weekly synthesis of what’s come in. Review the synthesis yourself first; after that, you can ask Claude to analyze the information to catch any significant points you may have overlooked.
练习：配置 Claude Cowork 来运行你的 MVP 阶段反馈循环：起草给早期用户列表的触达邮件、安排反馈会议、为错误报告和功能请求设计结构化的接收流程，并每周对所收到的内容进行综合总结。首先自己审阅这份总结；之后，你可以要求 Claude 分析这些信息，以捕捉你可能遗漏的任何重要点。
Iterate toward evidence, not toward completeness 朝着证据迭代，而不是朝着完整性The MVP stage ends when you have genuine evidence of product-market fit, no matter how “finished” the product feels. Declaring that you’ve achieved product-market fit and are now ready to move on from the MVP phase to the Launch stage is ultimately a judgement exercise that combines founder intuition with collected evidence. There are some useful litmus tests, though:
当你有产品-市场契合的真实证据时，最小可行产品阶段就结束了，无论产品感觉上有多“完成”。宣布你已经实现了产品-市场契合，并且现在准备好从 MVP 阶段进入发布阶段，这最终是一个结合了创始人直觉和收集到的证据的判断练习。不过，有一些有用的试金石：
The Sean Ellis test: Ask your active users: “How would you feel if you could no longer use this product?” If more than 40% answer “very disappointed,” that’s a meaningful PMF indicator. The effort test: Pre-product-market fit, retention requires constant intervention, including frequent outreach, incentives, personal follow-up, and a heroic founder energy expended to keep users engaged. Post product-market fit, the product starts doing that work on its own. When things begin pulling instead of pushing, that shift in effort is one of the clearest signals that something real has changed.
Sean Ellis 测试：询问你的活跃用户：“如果你不能再使用这个产品，你会感觉如何？”如果超过 40% 的人回答“非常失望”，那是一个有意义的产品-市场契合指标。努力测试：在产品-市场契合之前，留存需要持续的干预，包括频繁的触达、激励、个人跟进，以及创始人花费巨大的精力来保持用户参与。在产品-市场契合之后，产品开始自行完成这项工作。当事情开始由“推”转“拉”时，这种努力程度的转变是最清晰的信号之一，表明某些实质性的东西已经改变了。
Ultimately, no single data point confirms product-market fit because it’s a pattern that has to hold across multiple iteration cycles before you can definitively call it.
最终，没有任何一个单一的数据点能确认产品-市场契合，因为它是一种必须在多个迭代周期中持续存在的模式，之后你才能明确地称之为产品-市场契合。
Pivot when the evidence demands it 当证据要求时进行转型What if, even after investing all this work, you just can’t seem to arrive at product-market fit? The fact that your results don’t confirm the direction you started from is not failure, it’s the system working: the MVP stage is designed to surface this information before you over-invest in the wrong answer.
如果，即使投入了所有这些工作，你似乎仍然无法达到产品-市场契合呢？你的结果没有确认你最初的方向，这不是失败，而是系统在起作用：MVP 阶段的设计就是为了在你对错误答案过度投资之前揭示出这些信息。
When the data doesn’t support your current product, use Claude to work through what that data is telling you.
当数据不支持你当前的产品时，使用 Claude 来处理这些数据所告诉你的信息。
Exploring alternative customer segments. Perhaps the users who aren’t converting were never the right target to begin with. Often the right audience is already in your data, just underweighted.- Adjusting your product’s value prop. Maybe you have the correct audience but your MVP is just not resonating with users. An adjustment to onboarding, messaging, or core feature emphasis can potentially fix this without changing what you’ve built.
探索替代的客户群体。也许那些没有转化的用户从一开始就不是正确的目标。通常正确的受众已经在你的数据中了，只是权重不足。- 调整你的产品价值主张。也许你的受众是正确的，但你的 MVP 就是无法引起用户的共鸣。对入职流程、信息传递或核心功能重点的调整，有可能在不改变你已构建内容的情况下解决这个问题。
Stay open to the possibility that the disconnect may run deep enough to require a more fundamental change
对这样一种可能性保持开放：这种脱节可能足够深，以至于需要一个更根本性的改变
Exercise: If you’ve completed three or more iteration cycles without meaningful movement toward your product-market fit benchmarks, use Claude to run a diagnostic before deciding what to do next. Feed it your retention data, your user feedback, and your original problem hypothesis, and ask it three questions:
练习：如果你已经完成了三个或更多的迭代周期，但没有朝着你的产品-市场契合基准取得有意义的进展，那么在决定下一步做什么之前，使用 Claude 运行一次诊断。向其提供你的留存数据、用户反馈和你最初的问题假设，并问它三个问题：
Is there a segment in this data responding differently than the rest? Is the gap between designed value and experienced value a positioning problem or a product problem? What would have to be true for the current product to find genuine PMF, and is that scenario realistic given what you’re seeing?
在这组数据中，是否存在一个与其他部分反应不同的细分群体？设计价值与体验价值之间的差距是定位问题还是产品问题？要使当前产品找到真正的产品-市场契合，必须满足哪些条件，根据你目前所看到的情况，这种情景现实吗？
Let the answers determine whether you adjust, pivot, or return to the Idea stage.
让答案决定你是调整、转型，还是回到创意阶段。
5.Launch stage 发布阶段If the MVP stage was about proving your product deserves to exist, the Launch stage is about proving your business deserves to grow.
如果说 MVP 阶段是关于证明你的产品值得存在，那么发布阶段就是关于证明你的业务值得发展。
Launch stage goals 发布阶段目标In the Launch stage, startup founders must turn early traction into a repeatable, sustainable growth engine. Beyond making your product production-ready, you also must harden the infrastructure underneath it while simultaneously building an actual company around your product.
在发布阶段，创业创始人必须将早期的吸引力转化为可重复、可持续的增长引擎。除了让你的产品做好生产准备之外，你还必须强化其底层基础设施，同时在你的产品周围构建一个真正的公司。
Startups are naturally founder-centric during the Idea and MVP stages because you need the full situational awareness and tight feedback loops. Now, though, founders who still try to personally hold every thread become a Launch stage bottleneck. The goal isn’t to remove yourself from the company, but to build operational systems that free your attention for the decisions only a founder can make.
在创意和 MVP 阶段，初创企业天然是以创始人为中心的，因为你需要全面的情境感知和紧密的反馈循环。然而现在，那些仍然试图亲自把控每一条线索的创始人会成为发布阶段的瓶颈。目标不是让你自己离开公司，而是建立运营系统，将你的注意力解放出来，用于那些只有创始人才能做出的决策。
Launch stage exit criteria 发布阶段完成标准The Launch stage exit condition has three elements:
发布阶段的完成条件包含三个要素：
Growth is repeatable and channel-driven. You’re not just retaining users, you’re acquiring them predictably through specific channels with understood unit economics: CAC, LTV, and payback period are numbers you know and can defend. 增长是可重复且由渠道驱动的。你不仅在留存用户，而且在通过特定的渠道、以可预测的方式获取用户，并且理解其单位经济：客户获取成本、生命周期价值和回本周期是你了解且能够解释的数字。
The product can handle production workloads. Infrastructure is hardened, security and compliance are in order, and reliability holds under real production conditions (not just the conditions you tested for). 产品能够处理生产工作负载。基础设施已加固，安全性和合规性已就绪，可靠性在真实的生产条件下（而不仅仅是你测试过的条件下）能够保持。
Operations run without founder bottlenecks. Processes exist and automation is in place. You are no longer the person personally handling support, triage, sprint planning, or reporting. 运营在没有创始人瓶颈的情况下运行。流程存在，自动化已就位。你不再是亲自处理支持、分类、冲刺计划或报告的人。
Launch stage challenges 发布阶段挑战Finding product-market fit is the hardest problem in the early startup lifecycle. Now, the founder’s challenge becomes keeping it. The Launch stage is where companies that found real product traction may still fall apart if the organization that surrounds and supports the product can’t keep up. These are the failure modes to watch for.
找到产品-市场契合是早期创业生命周期中最难的问题。现在，创始人的挑战变成了保持它。在发布阶段，那些获得了真实产品吸引力的公司，如果围绕并支持产品的组织无法跟上，仍然可能分崩离析。以下是需要警惕的失败模式。
Technical debt comes due 技术债务到期The challenge: The MVP codebase built for speed and validation ran well enough to prove the product worked, but production traffic, new features, and growing complexity are now exposing the shortcuts.
挑战：为速度和验证而构建的 MVP 代码库运行得足够好，证明了产品有效，但现在生产流量、新功能和日益增长的复杂性正在暴露那些捷径。
At MVP, accumulating some technical debt was a reasonable tradeoff for velocity. In the Launch phase, that debt starts accruing interest, and the longer it goes unaddressed, the more expensive it is to fix.
在 MVP 阶段，积累一些技术债务是为了速度而做出的合理权衡。在发布阶段，这些债务开始产生利息，并且搁置的时间越长，修复的成本就越高。
The solution consists of a systematic architectural audit to identify structural weaknesses, targeted refactoring to address the worst of them, and a meaningful expansion of test coverage so that the next round of feature work doesn’t reintroduce the same problems.
解决方案包括：进行系统的架构审计以识别结构性弱点，进行有针对性的重构以解决其中最严重的问题，以及有意义地扩展测试覆盖率，以便下一轮功能工作不会重新引入相同的问题。
The founder becomes the bottleneck 创始人成为瓶颈The challenge: At MVP, the founder being in every loop was an asset. At Launch, as support volume grows, product decisions stack up, and operational complexity multiplies, that same instinct becomes the constraint.
挑战：在 MVP 阶段，创始人处于每个循环中是一种资产。在发布阶段，随着支持量的增长、产品决策的堆积以及运营复杂性的倍增，同样的本能变成了约束。
The transition from doing the work to designing the systems that do the work is one of the hardest shifts in the startup lifecycle. Because there’s rarely a clear moment when it happens, the risk is to miss it entirely and stay in builder mode while the organization stalls around you. Telltale signs that this is happening include decisions that should take an hour now take a week for you to get around to them, support requests that pile up because only you know the answer, and operational tasks that only happen when you personally remember to do them.
从做工作转变为设计完成工作的系统，是创业生命周期中最困难的转变之一。因为这种情况很少有一个清晰的时刻发生，风险在于你完全错过了它，并停留在构建者模式，而你周围的组织却停滞不前。这种情况发生的明显迹象包括：本应花费一小时的决定现在需要一周你才能抽出时间处理；支持请求堆积如山，因为只有你知道答案；运营任务只有当你亲自记得去做时才会发生。
The remedy is an all-out audit of everything you’re personally handling, from the tiniest task to the most high-stakes decisions, in order to identify what can be systematized, what can be delegated, and what genuinely still merits founder time and attention.
补救措施是对你亲自处理的所有事情（从最微小的任务到最高风险的决策）进行一次彻底的审计，以识别哪些可以系统化、哪些可以委派、哪些确实仍然值得创始人投入时间和注意力。
Security and compliance are no longer deferrable 安全与合规不再可以推迟The challenge: Keeping security and compliance measures simple was OK for MVP but now, with real users, real data, and potentially enterprise contracts on the table, it becomes a liability.
挑战：在 MVP 阶段保持安全和合规措施的简单性是可行的，但现在，面对真实用户、真实数据以及可能摆在桌面上的企业合同，这变成了一种责任。
At MVP, with a handful of beta users and no sensitive data in production, security vulnerabilities were theoretical risks. The hypothetical, however, becomes very real exposure risk the moment your product enters production with real users depending on it. Furthermore, compliance requirements that didn’t apply to a prototype definitely apply the moment you’re handling customer data, processing payments, or selling into regulated industries.
在 MVP 阶段，只有少数测试版用户且生产中没有敏感数据，安全漏洞是理论上的风险。然而，一旦你的产品进入生产环境，并有真实用户依赖它，这种假设就会变成非常真实的风险暴露。此外，那些不适用于原型的合规要求，在你处理客户数据、处理支付或向受监管行业销售的那一刻，就肯定适用了。
The remedy is a systematic security and compliance review before production scale arrives, not after, and treat everything that surfaces as a required remediation—not a suggestion—before the next wave of users arrives.
补救措施是在生产规模到来之前（而不是之后）进行系统的安全和合规审查，并将所有发现的问题视为必须的修复项（而不是建议），在下一次用户浪潮到来之前解决它们。
Expansion before you’re ready 在准备好之前扩张The challenge: New markets and funding opportunities look like growth opportunities. They can also be where product-market fit goes to die.
挑战：新市场和融资机会看起来像是增长机会。它们也可能是产品-市场契合走向消亡的地方。
The initial traction you’ve built is real, but it’s also specific to your early audience. Expanding too early into a market that’s meaningfully different from your original one introduces new user behaviors, compliance requirements, payment infrastructure, and baseline expectations that your product wasn’t designed around. Suddenly there are too many new variables and you lose the ability to interpret your own data clearly. You also run the risk of neglecting your original user base while chasing a new and unproven audience.
你建立的初始吸引力是真实的，但它也特定于你的早期受众。过早地扩展到一个与你最初市场有显著差异的市场，会引入新的用户行为、合规要求、支付基础设施以及你的产品未曾围绕其设计的基准期望。突然间出现了太多新的变量，你失去了清晰解读自己数据的能力。你还冒着在追逐一个新的、未经证实的受众的同时，忽视你原有用户基础的风险。
How Claude can help Launch stage founders Claude 如何在发布阶段帮助创始人All three forms of Claude are in full use in the Launch stage, and they support each other: each tool produces outputs that become inputs for the other two. The results compound organically, and a founder using all three tools together gets more than the sum of their parts.
Claude 的三种形态在发布阶段都被充分利用，并且它们相互支持：每个工具产生的输出都成为其他两个工具的输入。结果会有机地复合，同时使用这三种工具的创始人得到的效果大于各部分之和。
This is what makes the ultra-lean startup model structurally possible. When Claude Code builds the product, Claude Cowork builds the company around it, and Claude helps operationalize this product and organizational knowledge, a small team can run like a company nx its size.
这就是使超精益创业模式在结构上成为可能的原因。当 Claude Code 构建产品，Claude Cowork 围绕产品构建公司，而 Claude 帮助将产品和组织知识运营化时，一个小团队可以像一个规模数倍于它的公司一样运转。
Remediate technical debt before it compounds 在技术债务复利增长之前进行补救Your MVP codebase works, but it also needs a systematic remediation pass in search of any technical debt that could become a structural liability.
你的 MVP 代码库能工作，但它也需要一次系统的补救性审查，以寻找任何可能成为结构性负债的技术债务。
First, use Claude Code to run a full architectural audit: identify where the codebase is brittle, any shortcuts that will become expensive to maintain, and where test coverage is thin enough that the next round of feature work will reintroduce the same problems.
首先，使用 Claude Code 运行一次完整的架构审计：识别代码库中脆弱的地方、任何日后维护成本高昂的捷径，以及测试覆盖率薄弱到下一轮功能工作将重新引入相同问题的地方。
Feed Claude Code’s audit findings back to Claude to triage and sequence the remediation work: what needs to be fixed before the next release, what can wait a sprint, and what represents acceptable ongoing debt given your current stage. This is also the moment to document the architectural decisions you made during the MVP stage (the ones that lived in your head because there was no time to write them down). Getting them into a CLAUDE.md now ensures that every future Claude Code session starts from a shared understanding of how the system was designed and why.
将 Claude Code 的审计结果反馈给 Claude，以对补救工作进行分类和排序：哪些需要在下一个版本发布前修复，哪些可以等一个冲刺周期，以及哪些在当前阶段是可接受的持续性债务。这也是记录你在 MVP 阶段做出的架构决策（那些因为没时间写下来而只存在于你脑海中的决策）的时刻。现在将它们放入 CLAUDE.md 中，可以确保未来的每一次 Claude Code 会话都从一个关于系统如何设计以及为何如此设计的共同理解开始。
Exercise: Direct Claude Code to audit your MVP codebase and produce a prioritized list of structural weaknesses, test coverage gaps, and refactoring candidates. Then feed that list to Claude and ask it to sequence the remediation work across your several sprints: any significant issues that you need to address first, things that can be handled in parallel with feature development, and things that can wait.
练习：指示 Claude Code 审计你的 MVP 代码库，并生成一个按优先级排序的列表，列出结构性弱点、测试覆盖率差距以及需要重构的候选模块。然后将该列表提供给 Claude，要求它在你的几个冲刺周期中对补救工作进行排序：任何需要首先解决的重要问题、可以与功能开发并行处理的事项，以及可以等待的事项。
Build the systems that replace founder attention 构建取代创始人注意力的系统Building the operational systems that free your attention to handle responsibilities only the founder can tackle requires knowing exactly where your attention is going. Use Claude Cowork to run a structured audit of your current operational load, documenting every recurring task, every decision that lands on your desk, and every workflow that only happens because you personally remember to do it. Then have Claude Cowork categorize this inventory into what can be automated entirely, what needs a human but not necessarily you, and what genuinely requires founder judgment.
要构建能将你的注意力解放出来、以便处理只有创始人才能应对的责任的运营系统，你需要确切知道你的注意力去了哪里。使用 Claude Cowork 对你当前的运营负担进行一次结构化的审计，记录每一个重复性任务、每一个落在你办公桌上的决策，以及每一个只有因为你个人记得去做才会发生的工作流。然后让 Claude Cowork 将这个清单分类为：哪些可以完全自动化、哪些需要人但未必是你、哪些真正需要创始人判断。
Once the audit is complete, use Claude Cowork to design the workflow logic for the automation candidates: what triggers each workflow, what the decision rules are, what the output looks like, and where it goes when it’s done.
审计完成后，使用 Claude Cowork 为候选的自动化项目设计工作流逻辑：每个工作流的触发条件是什么、决策规则是什么、输出是什么样的，以及完成后它要去哪里。
Make security and compliance a product workstream 将安全与合规作为产品工作流的一部分Use Claude Code to surface code-level issues that frequently come up in SOC 2, GDPR, or HIPAA audits and standards that your target market requires. This will surface both vulnerabilities and compliance gaps. Feed those findings to Claude to help you prioritize the remediation work and design the controls, audit logging, and access management that enterprise buyers will ask for before they sign. Note: AI scans are an aid but not a substitute for qualified compliance review.
使用 Claude Code 来揭示你的目标市场所要求的 SOC 2、GDPR 或 HIPAA 审计和标准中经常出现的代码级别问题。这将同时揭示漏洞和合规差距。将这些发现提供给 Claude，以帮助你优先安排补救工作，并设计企业买家在签署合同之前会要求的控制措施、审计日志和访问管理。注意：人工智能扫描是一种辅助，但不能替代合格的合规审查。
Next, build the compliance workstream into your development cycle rather than running it as a one-time project; compliance documentation needs to be continually maintained and updated. For founders approaching enterprise contracts or international markets, this is also the moment where the Claude Code security scan can help you prepare for an independent security assessment.
接下来，将合规工作流构建到你的开发周期中，而不是将其作为一个一次性项目来运行；合规文档需要持续维护和更新。对于正在接触企业合同或国际市场的创始人来说，这也是 Claude Code 安全扫描可以帮助你为独立安全评估做准备的时候。
Exercise: Run a code-level security review with Claude Code oriented to the frameworks your target market requires. Feed the output to Claude and ask it to produce two things: a prioritized security remediation sequence and a list of the documentation and controls you’ll need to produce to satisfy a compliance review from a prospective enterprise buyer.
练习：使用 Claude Code 进行面向目标市场所需框架的代码级安全审查。将输出提供给 Claude，并要求它生成两样东西：一个按优先级排序的安全补救顺序列表，以及一份你需要生成以满足潜在企业买家合规审查的文档和控制措施清单。
Stand up the product management processes you’ve been skipping 建立你一直在跳过的产品管理流程The Launch stage requires a set of lightweight, repeatable processes that can run without requiring founder intervention to trigger or function. Use Claude to design how your product timeline and work cycles will be structured, what a spec needs to include before Claude Code touches a feature, how bug reports get triaged and routed, and what your weekly metrics report covers and how it’s distributed.
发布阶段需要一套轻量级、可重复的流程，这些流程可以在不需要创始人干预触发或运行的情况下运转。使用 Claude 来设计你的产品时间表和工作周期将如何构建、在 Claude Code 接触一个功能之前规格说明需要包含什么、错误报告如何被分类和分发，以及你的每周指标报告涵盖什么内容以及如何分发。
Once process design is done, use Claude Cowork to build and run the operational layer: scheduling sprint ceremonies, routing incoming bug reports to the right place, compiling weekly metrics from your connected data sources, and maintaining the feedback loop that keeps user signals flowing into product decisions.
流程设计完成后，使用 Claude Cowork 来构建和运行运营层：安排冲刺仪式、将传入的错误报告路由到正确的地方、从你连接的数据源编译每周指标，以及维护将用户信号持续输入产品决策的反馈循环。
Exercise: Ask Claude to design a lightweight product management operating system: a defined sprint cadence, a minimum spec template, a bug triage decision tree, and a weekly metrics brief that pulls from your actual data sources. Then set up Claude Cowork to implement and run the system’s recurring operational elements, like scheduling, routing, and report compilation, to happen on schedule without you.
练习：要求 Claude 设计一个轻量级的产品管理操作系统：一个定义的冲刺节奏、一个最小规格说明模板、一个错误分类决策树，以及一个从你实际数据源中提取数据的每周指标简报。然后设置 Claude Cowork 来实现和运行该系统中重复性的运营元素，如日程安排、路由和报告编译，使其无需你干预就能按计划发生。
6. Scale stage 规模化阶段During the Scale phase, the founder’s role re-centers from builder to public-facing executive. The product is still central, but your personal day-to-day work becomes increasingly about the company itself. Your attention must expand to new Scale-stage activities like analyst briefings and IPO roadshows even as you strive to maintain the lean, AI-centered structural advantage.
在规模化阶段，创始人的角色从构建者重新定位为面向公众的高管。产品仍然是核心，但你的个人日常工作越来越围绕着公司本身。即使你努力保持精益的、以人工智能为中心的结构性优势，你的注意力也必须扩展到新的规模化阶段活动，如分析师简报和 IPO 路演。
Scale stage goals 规模化阶段目标The work of scaling technical infrastructure keeps on going, and is now joined by the work of scaling the organization itself and maturing it into a business.
扩展技术基础设施的工作仍在继续，而现在又加入了扩展组织本身并使其成熟为一家企业的工作。
At the scale stage you’re looking at going from thousands of users to millions, and from one market to many. At every prior stage, growth was something you could feel your way through by being close to users and adjusting course based on data from tight feedback loops plus a healthy dose of founder instinct. Now, though, the goal is to build systematic growth that’s sustained by mature organizational operations.
在规模化阶段，你正着眼于从数千用户增长到数百万，从一个市场扩展到多个市场。在之前的每个阶段，增长是你可以通过接近用户、根据紧密反馈循环的数据以及加上一定量的创始人直觉来摸索前行的。然而现在，目标是建立由成熟的组织运营所维持的系统性增长。
For an AI-native startup, your goal should be to build a defensible moat through accumulated depth, stemming from the expertise you’ve built into your product, your product’s depth of integration with the other tools and platforms your users rely on, and the proprietary system data and workflows. The founders who’ve been building consistently in one direction, on consistent infrastructure, now have something genuinely hard to replicate.
对于一家人工智能原生初创企业，你的目标应该是通过积累的深度来构建一条可防御的护城河，这源于你融入产品的专业知识、你的产品与用户依赖的其他工具和平台的集成深度，以及专有的系统数据和工作流。那些一直在一个方向上、在一致的基础设施上持续构建的创始人，现在拥有了真正难以复制的东西。
At this stage, public investors, analysts, regulators, enterprise procurement teams, and acquirers apply greater pressure- along with greater skepticism because the stakes are higher now. Your product and org have to withstand external scrutiny: not just the capabilities of what you’ve built, but the governance, compliance posture, financial controls, and strategic narrative that surround it.
在这个阶段，公共投资者、分析师、监管机构、企业采购团队和收购方会施加更大的压力，同时也带有更大的怀疑，因为现在的赌注更高了。你的产品和组织必须经受住外部 scrutiny：不仅仅是你所构建的能力，还有围绕它的治理、合规姿态、财务控制和战略叙事。
Scale stage exit criteria 规模化阶段完成标准The exit condition at Scale is no longer a single milestone but a threshold event: the company is sustainable even as the founder is, increasingly, not directly running day-to-day operations. You’ve demonstrated systematic growth; built organizational governance and compliance infrastructure that satisfies the most demanding external reviewers; and have a solid answer to the question, “If a well-funded incumbent copied your product today, would your users stay?”
规模化阶段的完成条件不再是一个单一的里程碑，而是一个阈值事件：公司是可持续的，即使创始人越来越多地不再直接管理日常运营。你已经证明了系统性增长；建立了能够满足最苛刻外部审查者的组织治理和合规基础设施；并且对“如果一个资金充足的现有企业今天复制了你的产品，你的用户会留下吗？”这个问题有了坚实的答案。
In practice, this threshold will typically take one of three forms: sustainable profitability at a scale that no longer requires external capital, IPO-readiness, or acquisition. All three require that your growth is systematic and auditable, your product moat stands up under scrutiny, and your organization is operationally mature and sustainable.
在实践中，这个阈值通常表现为三种形式之一：在不再需要外部资本的规模上实现可持续盈利、达到 IPO 就绪状态，或被收购。这三者都要求你的增长是系统性和可审计的，你的产品护城河经得起 scrutiny，并且你的组织在运营上是成熟和可持续的。
When this is true, congratulations are in order: your startup has gone from being a bet to being a business.
当这一切成为现实时，值得祝贺：你的初创企业已经从一场赌博变成了一家企业。
Scale stage challenges 规模化阶段挑战Delegating the operational layer 委派运营层The challenge: Scale-stage operational systems have to run reliably and sustainably without being babysat. For a founder who has been hands-on since day one, that transition can be as much a psychological challenge as a structural one.
挑战：规模化阶段的运营系统必须可靠且可持续地运行，无需被时刻照看。对于一个从第一天起就亲力亲为的创始人来说，这种转变既是结构性的挑战，也是心理上的挑战。
Your Launch stage work was creating the systems; in the Scale phase, it becomes (1) maturing these systems until they are fully trustworthy and (2) then actually trusting them.
你在发布阶段的工作是创建系统；在规模化阶段，它变成了（1）使这些系统成熟，直到它们完全值得信赖，然后（2）真正地信任它们。
This is harder than it sounds. Even if you’re a founder who delegates well it’s not always obvious what to hand off and what to keep on your plate. Hand off too much, too fast—especially to AI-automated systems—and critical decisions get made without crucial context that only the founder can provide. Hold on too long, though, and you can become a bottleneck.
这比听起来要难。即使你是一个善于授权的创始人，也并非总是清楚哪些要交出去，哪些要留在自己盘子里。交出去太多、太快——尤其是交给人工智能自动化系统——关键的决策就会在没有只有创始人才能提供的关键背景信息的情况下做出。然而，握在手里太久，你又会成为一个瓶颈。
The fundamental challenge here is identifying the institutional knowledge that lives only in the founder’s head or undocumented workflows, and then codifying it into systems that are documented, auditable and transferable.
这里的基本挑战是识别那些只存在于创始人头脑中或未文档化的工作流中的机构知识，然后将其编纂成有文档记录、可审计和可转移的系统。
Scaling technical operations 扩展技术运营The challenge: Customers no longer evaluate only your product; they want to know that your organization can be a dependable infrastructure partner.
挑战：客户不再只评估你的产品；他们想知道你的组织能否成为一个可靠的基础设施合作伙伴。
Technical challenges during the first three startup stages centered on the codebase: building the right solution without accruing technical debt and then hardening security and compliance for real users. Having reached the Scale phase, the challenge now becomes everything built around the codebase; creating the support infrastructure, documentation, and reliability guarantees that signal maturity.
创业前三个阶段的挑战主要集中在代码库上：构建正确的解决方案而不积累技术债务，然后为真实用户强化安全和合规。到了规模化阶段，挑战现在变成了围绕代码库构建的一切：创建支持基础设施、文档和可靠性保证，以显示成熟度。
Larger-scale customers and institutional buyers signing multi-year contracts want these before they’ll sign, and they’ll also hold you to them once they do. The same AI infrastructure that got you this far, though, helps you build dedicated support functions with defined response times and documentation that a new customer’s engineering team can actually use.
更大规模的客户和签署多年合同的机构买家希望在签署之前看到这些，而且一旦签署，他们也会要求你遵守这些。然而，正是那个带你走到现在的人工智能基础设施，也能帮助你建立具有定义响应时间的专门支持功能，以及新客户工程团队真正能使用的文档。
Scaling organizational functions 扩展组织职能The challenge: A Scale-stage company generally needs organizational infrastructure like hiring, payroll, accounting, and legal operations, regardless of how many people are running it.
挑战：一家规模化阶段的公司通常需要组织基础设施，如招聘、工资单、会计和法律运营，无论有多少人在运营它。
At Launch, systematizing operations meant automating the workflows consuming founder attention. A Scale-stage startup now needs to grow an even broader, and in some ways more consequential, array of operational functions such as financial reporting, compliance monitoring, contract management, and customer support, to name a few.
在发布阶段，系统化运营意味着自动化消耗创始人注意力的工作流。规模化阶段的初创企业现在需要发展更广泛、在某些方面也更具影响力的运营职能，例如财务报告、合规监控、合同管理和客户支持，仅举几例。
Building a GTM function 构建市场进入（GTM）职能The challenge: Organic growth has a ceiling, and most Scale-stage founders hit it before they’ve ever had to build a real go-to-market function.
挑战：有机增长有一个天花板，而大多数规模化阶段的创始人都是在他们不得不构建真正的市场进入职能之前就撞上了这个天花板。
Idea, MVP, and Launch stage growth often originates from founder-led selling, from a well-timed Product Hunt post to personal relationships with early customers. Organic growth like this works only to a certain point, though, and most startups hit this limit in the Scale phase. Signs include flattening user curves, rising customer acquisition costs, and a pipeline that only moves when the founder is personally involved.
创意、MVP 和发布阶段的增长通常源于创始人主导的销售，从时机恰当的 Product Hunt 帖子到与早期客户的个人关系。然而，像这样的有机增长只在一定程度内有效，大多数初创企业在规模化阶段达到了这个极限。迹象包括用户曲线趋平、客户获取成本上升，以及只有创始人亲自参与时才会推进的销售管道。
Scale-stage growth requires building a dedicated growth engine to reach new and broader audiences for your product. Most startup founders, though, probably have never had to run things like marketing, sales, and analyst relations programs before. A legit GTM motion requires not just establishing new systems and processes, but also creating a brand voice and story for how you want to talk about your product. Because, at this stage in the startup lifecycle, you’re going to need one to reach not only individual new users, but also entire target audiences like investors and enterprise buyers.
规模化阶段的增长需要建立一个专门的增长引擎，以便为你的产品触达新的、更广泛的受众。然而，大多数创业创始人可能从未需要运行过市场营销、销售和分析师关系等项目。一个真正的市场进入动作不仅需要建立新的系统和流程，还需要为你想如何谈论你的产品创造品牌声音和故事。因为，在创业生命周期的这个阶段，你将需要一个这样的引擎，不仅要触达新的个人用户，还要触达整个目标受众，如投资者和企业买家。
Fortunately, the GTM function doesn’t have to be large to be effective, and the same AI infrastructure that built the product can bootstrap bringing it to market.
幸运的是，市场进入职能不一定要很大才能有效，而且构建了产品的同一个人工智能基础设施也可以助其进入市场。
How Claude can help Scale stage founders Claude 如何在规模化阶段帮助创始人Early startup stages use Claude as foundational infrastructure for the product itself: a research partner for validating the idea, the engineering team that designs and builds the prototype, and the AI operational layer that makes a single-founder startup possible. AI-native startup founders who reach the Scale
早期的创业阶段将 Claude 用作产品本身的基础设施：验证创意的研究伙伴、设计和构建原型的工程团队，以及使单人创始人初创企业成为可能的人工智能运营层。那些达到规模化阶段的人工智能原生初创企业创始人，
stage can now use Claude, Claude Code, and Claude Cowork to keep scaling the same way they built.
现在可以使用 Claude、Claude Code 和 Claude Cowork，以他们构建产品同样的方式来继续规模化。
Handling off day-to-day tasks to Claude Cowork 将日常任务移交给 Claude CoworkStart the Scale stage with a clear-eyed view of where you most need to invest your time and attention now, which can be a challenge for first time founders who’ve never built a business before. Claude can help by building the list of things only you should be doing at this stage, which could include things like product narrative decisions, board relationships, enterprise deals, and founder-to-founder conversations. Anything not on that list is a candidate for delegation or Claude Cowork automation.
以清醒的眼光开始规模化阶段，明确你现在最需要将时间和注意力投入到哪里，这对于从未建立过企业的初次创始人来说可能是一个挑战。Claude 可以帮助你列出只有你在这个阶段应该做的事情，其中可能包括产品叙事决策、董事会关系、企业交易和创始人之间的对话。任何不在这个清单上的事项都是可以委派或由 Claude Cowork 自动化的候选对象。
Exercise: Use Claude to produce a bottleneck map of your current operational layer: every workflow, decision, and approval currently routed through you. Now, ask Claude to extrapolate what happens to each one when you’re unavailable for a week. The workflows that stall are the ones where you are still hands-on enough to derail progress.
练习：使用 Claude 生成你当前运营层的瓶颈图谱：当前所有经由你的工作流、决策和审批。现在，要求 Claude 推断当你一周不在时，每个流程会发生什么。那些停滞不前的工作流，正是你仍然亲自插手到足以阻碍进展的地方。
How do these map to the inventory of founder priorities and responsibilities you made with Claude?
这些与你用 Claude 创建的创始人优先事项和责任清单如何对应？
Next, it’s time to pressure-test that the systems you’ve already built are actually ready to scale with your business as it grows.
接下来，是时候压力测试你已经建立的系统是否真的准备好随着业务的增长而扩展了。
Exercise: Use Claude to map your current workflows, and then ask it what happens to each one when you’re unavailable for a week. The workflows that stall are the ones where handoff criteria, escalation paths, or exception handling still need tightening. Claude can help analyze the failure points and recommend appropriate fixes so you can update or replace Claude Cowork automations as necessary.
练习：使用 Claude 绘制你当前的工作流，然后询问它当你一周不在时，每个工作流会发生什么。那些停滞不前的工作流，正是交接标准、升级路径或异常处理仍然需要收紧的地方。Claude 可以帮助分析失败点并推荐适当的修复措施，以便你可以根据需要更新或替换 Claude Cowork 的自动化。
Scale technical operations into enterprise-grade infrastructure 将技术运营扩展至企业级基础设施As you scale, buyers need reassurance that your product and your organization can be trusted as long-term infrastructure. Technical work still goes on inside the codebase as always, but now there is technical work around the codebase to handle, too.
随着你的扩展，买家需要 reassurance，即你的产品和你的组织可以作为一个值得信赖的长期基础设施。技术工作仍然像往常一样在代码库内部进行，但现在也有围绕代码库的技术工作需要处理。
The first step is to convert institutional knowledge into a system that scales. Use Claude to draft and maintain the written infrastructure that enterprise procurement expects to see, including product documentation, support playbooks, and SLAs.
第一步是将机构知识转化为一个可扩展的系统。使用 Claude 起草和维护企业采购部门期望看到的书面基础设施，包括产品文档、支持手册和服务水平协议。
In parallel, direct Claude Code to audit and harden the codebase against the specific reliability and security standards that enterprise contracts require, and to build out the technical support infrastructure that Discord-based community support never had to provide: logging, monitoring, incident response tooling, and the observability layer that makes SLAs actually enforceable.
同时，指示 Claude Code 根据企业合同要求的特定可靠性和安全标准来审计和加固代码库，并构建基于 Discord 的社区支持从未需要提供的技术支持基础设施：日志记录、监控、事件响应工具，以及使服务水平协议真正可执行的可观测层。
Claude Cowork then runs the operational layer of enterprise support itself: ticket routing, escalation workflows, documentation updates triggered by product changes, renewal tracking, and the reporting cadences that enterprise customer success relies on. Together, these three give a small team the support posture of a much larger organization, which is exactly what signing a multi-year enterprise contract requires you to demonstrate.
然后，Claude Cowork 运行企业支持本身的运营层：工单路由、升级工作流、由产品变更触发的文档更新、续约跟踪，以及企业客户成功所依赖的报告节奏。这三者共同为一个小型团队提供了远比其规模大得多的组织的支持姿态，而这正是签署多年企业合同所需要你展示的。
Exercise: Pick your three most demanding prospects or identify three ideal customers for your product that you’d love to sign. Ask Claude to produce a gap analysis: what documentation, SLAs, and support infrastructure would an enterprise procurement team at each of these accounts expect to see before signing a multi-year contract, and where do you currently fall short? Use the output to sequence the technical and documentation work across Claude Code and Claude Cowork.
练习：挑选你三个要求最高的潜在客户，或者为你产品识别出三个你很想签下的理想客户。要求 Claude 生成一个差距分析：这些客户中的每一个，其企业采购团队在签署多年合同之前期望看到哪些文档、服务水平协议和支持基础设施，而你目前在哪些方面存在不足？使用输出结果来安排跨 Claude Code 和 Claude Cowork 的技术和文档工作的顺序。
Build a real GTM function 构建真正的市场进入（GTM）职能Founder hustle got you this far, but scaling your startup requires creating and implementing an actual go-to-market strategy. AI can help you build, then and run, that complete GTM engine.
创始人的拼搏精神让你走到了这一步，但扩展你的初创企业需要创建并实施一个真正的市场进入策略。人工智能可以帮助你构建，然后运行，那个完整的市场进入引擎。
Claude can assist with building foundational GTM resources from scratch: market segmentation, messaging architecture, analyst relations strategy, sales playbooks, and the investor-facing metrics narratives that matter once you’re talking to public investors, enterprise buyers, and Wall Street analysts. Each of these audiences has its own vocabulary and evaluates you against its own standards; Claude’s job is to translate your product’s value props into a product marketing approach that’s relevant for each audience segment.
Claude 可以帮助你从零开始构建基础的市场进入资源：市场细分、信息传递架构、分析师关系策略、销售手册，以及当你与公共投资者、企业买家 and 华尔街分析师交谈时重要的、面向投资者的指标叙事。这些受众中的每一个都有自己的词汇，并根据自己的标准来评估你；Claude 的工作是将你产品的价值主张转化为一种对每个受众细分群体都具有相关性的产品营销方法。
Now, Claude Cowork can become your tactical execution layer: content pipelines, outbound sequences, analyst briefing logistics, newsroom and PR cadences, CRM hygiene, pipeline reporting, and the many recurring cycles that turn GTM strategy into actual commercial motion.
现在，Claude Cowork 可以成为你的战术执行层：内容管道、外向序列、分析师简报后勤、新闻编辑室和公关节奏、客户关系管理系统卫生、管道报告，以及将市场进入策略转化为实际商业动作的许多重复循环。
Where the GTM motion requires product marketing infrastructure—interactive demo environments, integration documentation, sandbox tenants, API references, technical one-pagers—Claude Code can build it for you. Buyers expect to evaluate your product technically and, in the Scale phase, a Loom video and a sales deck no longer suffice. This is also the infrastructure that lets your GTM motion run asynchronously: a well-built demo environment closes deals while you’re in board meetings.
在市场进入动作需要产品营销基础设施的地方——交互式演示环境、集成文档、沙箱租户、API 参考、技术单页——Claude Code 可以为你构建它们。买家期望对你的产品进行技术评估，而在规模化阶段，一个 Loom 视频和一个销售演示文稿已经不够了。这也是让你的市场进入动作能够异步运行的基础设施：一个精心构建的演示环境可以在你参加董事会会议时促成交易。
Turning domain expertise and institutional knowledge into AI context 将领域专业知识和机构知识转化为人工智能背景信息Many ultra-lean startup founders are building highly specific apps or tools for a real-world problem they experience or observe first-hand in a particular sector. Agentic AI now makes it possible for founders who have never written a line of code to use their domain expertise to build products that solve sophisticated problems. Claude, Claude Code, and Claude Cowork each play a part in converting founder knowledge into compounding product specificity.
许多超精益的创业创始人正在为他们在特定领域亲身经历或观察到的现实问题构建高度特定的应用或工具。智能体人工智能现在使得从未写过一行代码的创始人能够利用他们的领域专业知识来构建解决复杂问题的产品。Claude、Claude Code 和 Claude Cowork 各自在将创始人知识转化为复合的产品特异性方面发挥作用。
Using Claude to capture, organize, and refine founder knowledge puts domain expertise somewhere the product can reach. Through extended conversations, projects, and memory, a founder can share everything they know—industry jargon, regulatory gotchas, edge cases, frustrations, reasons why the obvious answers to this problem don’t work—into a structured, searchable context. Skills can then codify recurring workflows (e.g., “how I audit a commercial lease,” “how I triage a patient intake form”) into reusable routines Claude runs the same way every time. Over months, this becomes a proprietary knowledge substrate that no generalist AI can match.
使用 Claude 来捕获、组织和提炼创始人知识，将领域专业知识置于产品可以触及的地方。通过扩展的对话、项目和记忆，创始人可以将他们知道的一切——行业术语、监管陷阱、边缘案例、挫折、为什么这个问题的明显答案行不通的原因——分享到一个结构化的、可搜索的背景中。然后，技能可以将重复出现的工作流（例如，“我如何审计一份商业租约”、“我如何对患者入院表进行分类”）编纂成可重复使用的程序，Claude 每次都按同样的方式运行。几个月后，这就变成了一个任何通用人工智能都无法比拟的专有知识基础。
Externalizing your domain knowledge with Claude becomes invaluable for encoding industry-specific edge cases into your product: a generalist AI medical billing tool breaks on 340B drug program claims, for example, but yours has specific logic for them. Claude Code helps you translate common frustrations experienced by other professionals in your field into validation logic, prompt refinements, or an MCP integration with a niche industry system your competitors haven’t heard of. As a result, your app or tool’s depth and breadth both continually compound in a way that competitors simply can’t replicate.
用 Claude 将你的领域知识外化，对于将特定行业的边缘案例编码到你的产品中变得非常有价值：例如，一个通用的人工智能医疗账单工具会在 340B 药品计划索赔上出错，但你的产品有针对它们的特定逻辑。Claude Code 帮助你将在你所在领域的其他专业人士遇到的常见挫折转化为验证逻辑、提示词优化，或与你的竞争对手闻所未闻的小众行业系统进行 MCP 集成。其结果是，你的应用或工具的深度和广度都以竞争对手根本无法复制的方式持续复合增长。
Exercise: Identify one edge case a generic competitor would definitely get wrong in your vertical. Work with Claude Code to build a dedicated test case for it (not a unit test) based on a scenario you’ve actually seen. Every time a similar edge case surfaces, add it. Your test suite becomes a map of your moat.
练习：识别出一个在你的垂直领域中，通用竞争对手肯定会处理错误的边缘案例。与 Claude Code 合作，基于你实际见过的场景为其构建一个专门的测试用例（不是单元测试）。每次出现类似的边缘案例时，都将其添加进去。你的测试套件就变成了你护城河的地图。
Compound accumulated user data into a defensible advantage 将累积的用户数据复合为可防御的优势As users interact with your product, they generate behavioral signals (i.e., which outputs they accept and which they reject), which informs the product roadmap. Over time, you’ll learn the specific patterns, preferences, and edge cases of your particular user base. This is what we mean by compounding value: each improvement makes the product more useful, which drives more usage, which creates more feedback, which drives more improvement.
当用户与你的产品交互时，他们会生成行为信号（例如，他们接受哪些输出，拒绝哪些输出），这些信号为产品路线图提供信息。随着时间的推移，你将了解你特定用户群体的具体模式、偏好和边缘案例。这就是我们所说的复合价值：每一次改进都使产品更有用，从而推动更多的使用，这又产生更多的反馈，进而推动更多的改进。
This data is time-locked, context-specific, and impossible for a copycat to recreate: you simply can’t buy the behavioral fingerprint of thousands of users who’ve been refining their workflows inside your product.
这些数据是时间锁定的、特定于上下文的，并且是模仿者无法重现的：你根本无法买到成千上万用户在你的产品内部不断优化其工作流程的行为指纹。
Claude can help audit whatever user interaction data you’ve collected, identify the highest-signal behavioral patterns within it, and design the feedback loop that turns ongoing usage into systematic model improvement.
Claude 可以帮助审计你收集到的任何用户交互数据，识别其中信号最强的行为模式，并设计将持续使用转化为系统性模型改进的反馈循环。
Exercise: Feed Claude a summary of your product’s interaction data: what you’ve been collecting, how long you’ve been collecting it, and what you know about how users engage with your product over time. Ask it to identify the three highest-signal behavioral patterns in that data and design a feedback loop that turns each one into a systematic model improvement. Then ask it to help you draft a one-page moat narrative to inform product marketing: the story of how your data flywheel works, how long it’s been spinning, and why a well-resourced competitor starting today couldn’t replicate it in under two years.
练习：向 Claude 提供你的产品交互数据的摘要：你一直在收集什么数据、收集了多长时间，以及你对用户如何随着时间的推移与你的产品互动所了解的情况。要求它识别出该数据中信号最强的三个行为模式，并设计一个反馈循环，将每个模式转化为系统性的模型改进。然后要求它帮助你起草一份一页纸的护城河叙事，用于产品营销：你的数据飞轮如何运作的故事、它已经旋转了多长时间，以及为什么一个从今天开始的资源充足的竞争对手无法在两年内复制它。
Create workflow lock-in 创建工作流锁定Compounding data network effects make your product harder to replicate, but user workflow lock-in makes your product harder to leave. The longer users run your product inside their daily operations, the more deeply it gets embedded in how they actually work. They’ve built automations on top of it, trained people to use it, and connected it to their data sources and other tools. The prompts they’ve developed, the workflows they’ve refined, and the outputs they’ve standardized have all been shaped around what your product does and how it does it. At this point, switching goes from product decision to full scale operational project.
复合的数据网络效应使你的产品更难被复制，但用户工作流锁定使你的产品更难被离开。用户在日常运营中使用你的产品时间越长，它就越深入地嵌入到他们实际的工作方式中。他们基于你的产品构建了自动化，培训了人员使用它，并将其连接到他们的数据源和其他工具。他们开发的提示词、他们优化的工作流以及他们标准化的输出，都是围绕着你的产品做什么以及如何做而形成的。在这一点上，切换就从一个产品决策变成了一个全面的运营项目。
The first step in creating workflow lock-in is asking Claude to map your current customer base by integration depth. For each customer segment, identify what workflows they’ve built on top of your product and which integrations they depend on. This shows where your product is sticking, and where it needs to go deeper.
创建工作流锁定的第一步是要求 Claude 按集成深度绘制你当前的客户群。对于每个客户细分群体，识别他们在你的产品之上构建了哪些工作流，以及他们依赖哪些集成。这显示了你的产品在哪些地方被粘住了，以及哪些地方需要更深入。
The more integrations you offer, the more surface area a customer has to construct workflows that rely on your product. Claude Code helps you quickly spin up native integrations with the data pipelines, project management tools, and other systems that your target users depend on. Claude Code can also build the APIs, webhooks, and SDKs that let customers not just use your product, but build on top of it—the deepest form of lock-in
你提供的集成越多，客户构建依赖你产品的工作流的表面积就越大。Claude Code 帮助你快速创建与目标用户依赖的数据管道、项目管理工具和其他系统的原生集成。Claude Code 还可以构建 API、webhook 和 SDK，让客户不仅使用你的产品，还可以在其之上进行构建——这是最深的锁定形式。
Exercise: Ask Claude to help you build a workflow integration audit for your top ten customers. For each one, document the automations they’ve built, the integrations they depend on, the team workflows that run through your product, and your estimate of their switching cost. Then ask Claude to identify the patterns across the group: what types of integration create the deepest lock-in for your specific product, and what you could build or enable to deepen integration for customers who are currently at the surface.
练习：要求 Claude 帮助你为你的前十大客户构建一个工作流集成审计。对于每个客户，记录他们构建的自动化、他们依赖的集成、通过你的产品运行的团队工作流，以及你对他们转换成本的估计。然后要求 Claude 识别该群体中的模式：对于你的特定产品，哪些类型的集成创造了最深的锁定，以及你可以构建或启用什么来加深目前处于表层的客户的集成。
7. Same job, new rules 相同工作，新规则In the AI area, the founder’s job hasn’t changed: find a real problem, build something that solves it, and scale it into a company that matters. What’s changed is the path to get there. Across the four stages—Idea, MVP, Launch, and Scale—AI compresses quarters into weeks.
在人工智能时代，创始人的工作没有改变：找到一个真实的问题，构建一个解决它的东西，并将其扩展为一家有意义的公司。改变的是实现它的路径。在这四个阶段——创意、最小可行产品、发布和规模化——人工智能将季度压缩成了数周。
Validation cycles that used to take months now take afternoons. A working prototype no longer requires a co-founder with the right stack; it requires a clear problem and a few focused sessions with a coding agent. Launch readiness compresses from a pre-launch scramble into a continuous workstream. And at scale, the operational weight that used to force early hires into firefighting roles can increasingly be handed off to AI, freeing your team to spend their attention on the judgment calls that become your moat.
过去需要数月的验证周期现在只需一个下午。一个可工作的原型不再需要一个拥有合适技术栈的联合创始人；它需要一个清晰的问题以及与一个编码智能体进行几次专注的对话。发布就绪从发布前的忙乱压缩为一个持续的工作流。而在规模化阶段，过去迫使早期员工扮演救火队员角色的运营负担，可以越来越多地移交给人工智能，从而解放你的团队，让他们将注意力投入到那些成为你护城河的判断性决策上。
The bottlenecks are no longer what you can build, but what you choose to build.
瓶颈不再是你能构建什么，而是你选择构建什么。
Resources 资源Building with Claude 与 Claude 一起构建 Building with Claude- Building AI Agents for Startups: Shares how startups use agents to become less founder-dependent as they scale. 与 Claude 一起构建 - 为初创企业构建人工智能智能体：分享了初创企业如何随着规模扩大而使用智能体来减少对创始人的依赖。 Claude Code docs: Carries builders from initial installation to advanced agentic workflows. Pro-tip: get started with the “How Claude Code works” overview. Claude Code 文档：将构建者从初始安装带到高级智能体工作流。专业提示：从“Claude Code 工作原理”概述开始。 Claude Code best practices: Covers patterns that have worked inside Anthropic and across engineering teams — context management, permissions, planning, and verification workflows. Claude Code 最佳实践：涵盖了在 Anthropic 内部和各个工程团队中行之有效的模式——上下文管理、权限、规划和验证工作流。 Using CLAUDE.md files: Walks through how to configure Claude Code for your specific codebase. Essential reading for MVP-stage founders setting up their development environment. 使用 CLAUDE.md 文件：逐步介绍如何为你的特定代码库配置 Claude Code。对于设置开发环境的 MVP 阶段创始人来说，这是必读内容。 Claude Code power user tips: Highlights workflow patterns from the Claude Code team itself, including parallel sessions and verification loops. Claude Code 高级用户技巧：重点介绍了来自 Claude Code 团队本身的工作流模式，包括并行会话和验证循环。 Get started with Claude Cowork: Shares how teams can set up Claude Cowork and start implementing skills, plugins, and other features that scale its impact across your startup. 开始使用 Claude Cowork：分享团队如何设置 Claude Cowork 并开始实施技能、插件和其他功能，以扩展其对初创企业的影响。 Tutorials: claude.com/resources/tutorials offers a searchable list of hands-on walkthroughs for specific tasks. 教程：claude.com/resources/tutorials 提供了针对特定任务的可搜索的动手演练列表。 Founder stories 创始人的故事 How three YC startups built their companies with Claude Code: Examining how HumanLayer (F24), Ambral (W25), and Vulcan Technologies (S25) used Claude to get prototypes to market fast and scale AI-powered platforms with agentic coding workflows. 三家 YC 初创企业如何用 Claude Code 构建公司：研究了 HumanLayer (F24)、Ambral (W25) 和 Vulcan Technologies (S25) 如何使用 Claude 快速将原型推向市场，并利用智能体编码工作流扩展人工智能驱动的平台。 GC AI’s founders used domain expertise to build a responsive, Claude-powered legal platform for how in-house teams actually work: company-specific playbooks, cross-functional stakeholders, and variable risk tolerance thresholds GC AI 的创始人利用领域专业知识，为内部团队的实际工作方式构建了一个响应式的、由 Claude 驱动的法律平台：针对特定公司的剧本、跨职能利益相关者以及可变的风险容忍度阈值。 Carta Healthcare uses Claude to power their clinical abstraction platform, processing 22,000 surgical cases per year and reducing data abstraction time by 66%. Carta Healthcare 使用 Claude 为其临床抽象平台提供动力，每年处理 22,000 例外科手术，并将数据抽象时间减少 66%。 Anything, powered by Claude and the Agent SDK, has helped 1.5 million users turn ideas into working software products without writing code, including a non-technical founder who built and is already selling a full recruiting platform. Anything’s AI agent handles the full build so solopreneurs can double down on their domain expertise. Anything 由 Claude 和 Agent SDK 驱动，已帮助 150 万用户无需编写代码就能将想法转化为可工作的软件产品，其中包括一位非技术型创始人，他构建并已经在销售一个完整的招聘平台。Anything 的人工智能智能体处理整个构建过程，因此个人创业者可以加倍专注于他们的领域专业知识。 Cogent is an applied AI lab building agents to automate critical enterprise security tasks. The startup uses Claude as the reasoning layer for agents that automate investigation, prioritization, and remediation across the full vulnerability lifecycle. Cogent 是一个应用人工智能实验室，构建智能体来自动化关键的企业安全任务。这家初创公司使用 Claude 作为智能体的推理层，这些智能体在整个漏洞生命周期中自动化调查、优先级排序和修复。 Airtree uses Claude Cowork as the center of its operations infrastructure, uniting data that used to be scattered across a dozen different tools and teams. Now, when one person builds a workflow automation with skills, everyone in the organization can use it to do all the things on their to-do list that never got done. Airtree 使用 Claude Cowork 作为其运营基础设施的中心，将曾经分散在十几个不同工具和团队中的数据统一起来。现在，当一个人用技能构建一个工作流自动化时，组织中的每个人都可以使用它来执行待办事项列表中那些从未完成的事情。 Duvo builds AI agents that run procurement, supply chain, and category management processes across ERPs, supplier portals, spreadsheets, email, and even phone calls. Duvo is built entirely on Claude, using the Agent SDK to orchestrate across workflows. Duvo 构建了能够跨企业资源规划系统、供应商门户、电子表格、电子邮件甚至电话运行采购、供应链和品类管理流程的人工智能智能体。Duvo 完全构建在 Claude 之上，使用 Agent SDK 来编排跨工作流的操作。 Zingage is an AI agent platform built for 24/7 automated operations for home-care agencies. The startup uses Claude’s structured tool calling to orchestrate across an EMR and multiple communication channels, and Claude’s contextual reasoning to build agents that can give nuanced, patient-tailored outcomes rather than pattern-matching to the most common response. Zingage 是一个为家庭护理机构提供 24/7 自动化运营而构建的人工智能智能体平台。这家初创公司使用 Claude 的结构化工具调用来编排跨电子病历和多个通信通道的操作，并利用 Claude 的上下文推理来构建能够提供细致的、针对患者的结果的智能体，而不是模式匹配最常见的回答。 Kindora is an AI-powered platform built by a nonprofit executive who used Claude Sonnet to build a desperately-needed tool for intelligently matching charities with funders. After filtering thousands of matches down to the few worth pursuing, Kindora’s MCP connector lets nonprofits access its prospecting tools directly within Claude. Kindora 是一个由人工智能驱动的平台，由一位非营利组织高管构建，他使用 Claude Sonnet 构建了一个急需的工具，用于智能地将慈善机构与资助者匹配。在将数千个匹配项筛选到少数值得追求的之后，Kindora 的 MCP 连接器让非营利组织可以直接在 Claude 内部访问其勘探工具。 Wordsmith was founded by a lawyer-turned-CTO to provide reliable AI-powered legal technology for in-house legal teams. Claude is the reasoning engine for Wordsmith’s contract review, agreement drafting, and document review capabilities, and the startup’s engineering team uses Claude Code for building and evolving the platform itself. Wordsmith 由一位律师转型为首席技术官的人创立，旨在为内部法律团队提供可靠的人工智能驱动的法律技术。Claude 是 Wordsmith 合同审查、协议起草和文档审查功能的推理引擎，该初创企业的工程团队使用 Claude Code 来构建和发展平台本身。 Startup support and opportunities 初创企业支持与机会 Anthropic Startups Program: For startups working with Anthropic’s VC partners, the program provides free API credits, the highest tier of publicly available rate limits, and invitations to exclusive founder events and workshops. Anthropic 初创企业计划：对于与 Anthropic 的风险投资合作伙伴合作的初创企业，该计划提供免费的 API 额度、公开可用的最高等级速率限制，以及独家创始人活动和研讨会的邀请。 Claude community: Forums and community spaces for builders. Claude 社区：面向构建者的论坛和社区空间。 Live learning resources: Conferences, webinars, livestreams, and recordings. 实时学习资源：会议、网络研讨会、直播和录像。
